title: Service Breaker
module_title: Exploiting Multiple Services for Lateral Movement
prerequisites: Service exploitation basics (FTP, SMB, SSH, Telnet)
learning_objectives: Identify exploitable services on network hosts
Execute exploitation chains across multiple servers
Perform lateral movement using compromised systems
scenario: 4 servers with vulnerable services. Gain access to each through its exposed service, then chain them for lateral movement.

scenario_context.setting: Network of interconnected servers with varying service configurations
scenario_context.challenge: Chain exploits across multiple services to achieve full network compromise
scenario_context.relevance: Demonstrates how initial foothold can lead to full infrastructure compromise

subtasks.title: FTP Server Access
subtasks.description: Test anonymous access, default creds, version CVEs.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Enumerate FTP service. Test anonymous login. Try default credentials. Check for known version vulnerabilities. Identify accessible files and directory structure.
subtasks.evidence_required: FTP access evidence with file listing

subtasks.title: SMB Exploitation
subtasks.description: Exploit null sessions, default shares, known vulns.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Test SMB null session access. Enumerate shares. Try known exploits (MS17-010, etc.). Document access path.
subtasks.evidence_required: SMB exploitation log with share access

subtasks.title: SSH/Telnet Access
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Identify SSH/Telnet versions. Test for known vulnerabilities. Attempt credential reuse from previous compromises. Gain shell access.

subtasks.title: Vulnerability Chaining
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use credentials/access from first service to compromise second server. Chain exploits across all 4 servers. Map interdependencies.
subtasks.evidence_required: Attack chain diagram showing service relationships

subtasks.title: Access Log & Evidence
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Document every access point, timestamp, credentials used, data accessed. Create detailed progression timeline.

subtasks.title: Findings Report
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Write findings report with executive summary, methodology, findings per service, and remediation. Include chain diagram.

resources.name: SSH Version Fingerprinting and CVE Matching

reflection_prompt: How did compromising one service enable access to others? What patterns indicate services share infrastructure?

quiz.question: Why is credential reuse a critical vulnerability in lateral movement?
options: Systems often share credentials across services
quiz.explanation: When the same credentials are used across multiple systems, compromising one service grants access to others automatically.

quiz.question: What is the primary goal of service chaining in a network attack?
options: Achieve complete infrastructure compromise through sequential exploitation
quiz.explanation: Service chaining uses initial access to pivot through the network, progressively compromising more critical systems.

mentor_notes: Assess logical progression of exploits and quality of chain documentation. Report should show understanding of infrastructure relationships. 48-hour deadline requires efficient testing.
